title: henckelia walkerae
intro: Henckelia walkerae, also known as the Khasi hill gentian, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Henckelia walkerae include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Henckelia walkerae is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk remedies and Ayurvedic medicine.
